name: Pituitary Gland
location: Seated in the sella turcica of the sphenoid bone at the base of the brain.
description: The 'Master Gland' of the endocrine system, secreting hormones that regulate growth, blood pressure, reproduction, metabolism, and stress response under hypothalamic control.
functions: Growth hormone (GH) secretion for tissue repair
clinicalImportance: Compression of the optic chiasm by a growing pituitary macroadenoma causes classic bitemporal hemianopsia (tunnel vision).
researchNotes: Transsphenoidal endonasal endoscopic surgery allows minimally invasive resection of pituitary tumors through the nasal cavity.
interestingFacts: Only the size of a pea (approx 0.5g), yet controls almost all bodily endocrine glands.
